frameworks.name: three-layer convolutional network model
frameworks.one_line: deep network fit to retinal ganglion responses under natural scenes

frameworks.name: predictive coding
frameworks.one_line: model reproduces and generates new hypotheses for predictive retinal phenomena

frameworks.name: interneuron decomposition
frameworks.one_line: decomposes ganglion computations into contributions of model interneurons

frameworks.name: ethological / natural-scene neuroscience
frameworks.one_line: frames retinal code in terms of natural visual computation

core_question: What circuit mechanisms underlie the retinal neural code for natural scenes?
key_assumptions: a three-layer network captures retinal computation under natural input
model interneurons correspond to real recorded interneurons
natural-scene fitting suffices to reproduce diverse retinal phenomena
manipulations: natural-scene stimuli used to fit the model
comparison of model interneurons to separately recorded interneurons
decomposition of ganglion outputs into interneuron contributions
key_findings: model predicts retinal natural-scene responses near experimental limits
model interneurons are highly correlated with separately recorded interneurons
natural-scene-fit models reproduce motion encoding, adaptation, and predictive coding
decomposition yields new hypotheses for how interneurons combine into retinal computations
interpretations: interpretable deep models reveal circuit mechanisms of ethological retinal computation
interneuron combinations can explain predictive phenomena currently lacking mechanism
